text: To the woman herself, according to the Latin version; to the whole country, according to the Hebrew text (cf. Deut. 24.4).

Literally, ‘Ever since the days of our youth shame has devoured the earnings of our fathers, their flocks and herds, sons and daughters’. But it is most probable that the word ‘shame’ stands here as a pious substitute for the name of the god Baal.
